title: Broad risk-off close — QQQ leads lower as VIX spikes into heavy earnings
tag: All four major ETFs closed red with QQQ the weakest and volatility jumping sharply — a day to protect capital amid one of the busiest earnings sessions of the quarter.

spy.read: Pulled back further from the 52-week high — broad risk-off tape

qqq.read: Clear laggard again — mega-cap tech led the market lower

dia.read: Fell in line with the broader tape — no defensive rotation today

iwm.read: Held up best of the four — small caps relatively resilient

sentiment.label: BEARISH
sentiment.qualifier: broad decline across all four major ETFs with volatility spiking

weather.volatility: VIX 18.70, up 12.38% on the day — fear premium jumped sharply
weather.tone: Protect-first — steep down day with a volatility spike; wait for the tape to stabilize

cluster: Leadership lane: IWM held up best; QQQ was the clear laggard — broad-based weakness, not a single-sector story
Volatility regime: VIX jumped to 18.70 (+12.38%) — treat signals as lower-confidence until vol cools
Breadth read: all four major ETFs closed red — a broad risk-off day
Heavy earnings tape: Intel, T-Mobile, Union Pacific, Honeywell, Comcast, Lockheed Martin, Blackstone, and Southwest Airlines were among today's reporters
Rotation note: no clear rotation into safety — value, growth, and small caps all fell together
Behavior rule: no chasing downside; keep size small, demand A+ setups, and respect wider stops given the vol spike
account: Mission for today: protect capital — reduce size and wait for a cleaner tape before adding risk
Broker status: IB sandbox scoped, not yet connected; Schwab pending
Event risk: earnings season remains in full swing — more large-cap reports land through the rest of the week
checklist: Execution reality check: cash is safety — no obligation to trade a volatile, broad-red tape
Core universe glance: SPY/QQQ/DIA/IWM all red; QQQ weakest, IWM steadiest
Day-specific catalyst note #1: Intel, T-Mobile, Union Pacific, Honeywell, Comcast, Lockheed Martin, Blackstone, and Southwest Airlines reported today
Day-specific catalyst note #2: VIX spiked over 12% — treat any new entries as lower-confidence until volatility settles
body_muted: Today is a protect-first day. A volatility spike like this calls for patience, not new risk.
_sources: SPY/QQQ/DIA/IWM/VIX quotes via finance_quotes; overall sentiment via finance_market_sentiment; earnings items via finance_earnings_schedule (Perplexity Realtime Finance Data). Account/checklist lines are Plex's draft judgment calls for Noal to adjust.
